zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley於1427年出生在蘇格蘭雷福福爾達利時，她的父親Alan Stewart 2nd Lord of Aubigny and Concressault20歲，母親Lady Catherine Seton17歲。她於1444年3月29日與George Campbell 13th of Loudoun在蘇格蘭艾斯爾盧度結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。她去世於1494年3月9日在她的家鄉享年67歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley于1427年出生在苏格兰雷福福爾達利时，她的父亲Alan Stewart 2nd Lord of Aubigny and Concressault20岁，母亲Lady Catherine Seton17岁。她于1444年3月29日与George Campbell 13th of Loudoun在苏格兰艾斯爾盧度结婚。他们至少是5个儿子的父母。她去世于1494年3月9日在她的家乡享年67岁。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley was born in 1427, in Darnley, Renfrewshire, Scotland, her father, Alan Stewart 2nd Lord of Aubigny and Concressault, was 20 and her mother, Lady Catherine Seton, was 17. She married George Campbell 13th of Loudoun on 29 March 1444, in Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. She died on 9 March 1494, in her hometown, at the age of 67.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley est née en 1427, à Darnley, Renfrewshire, Écosse, son père, Alan Stewart 2nd Lord of Aubigny and Concressault, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Lady Catherine Seton, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé George Campbell 13th of Loudoun le 29 mars 1444, à Loudoun, Ayrshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils. Elle est décédée le 9 mars 1494, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 67 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley 1427 in Darnley, Renfrewshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Alan Stewart 2nd Lord of Aubigny and Concressault 20 und ihre Mutter Lady Catherine Seton 17. Sie hat am 29. März 1444 in Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland George Campbell 13th of Loudoun geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne. Sie starb am 9. März 1494 im Alter von 67 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley è nata il nel 1427 nel Darnley, Renfrewshire, Scotland, sua padre, Alan Stewart 2nd Lord of Aubigny and Concressault, aveva 20 anni e sua madre, Lady Catherine Seton, aveva 17 anni. Lei sposato George Campbell 13th of Loudoun il 29 marzo 1444, nel Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli. Morì il 9 marzo 1494, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 67 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Stewart of Darnleyが1427年にスコットランドレンフリューシュアイアダルンリーで生まれた時，父Alan Stewart 2nd Lord of Aubigny and Concressaultは20歳，母Lady Catherine Setonは17歳であった。1444年3月29日にスコットランドエイルシュアイアロウドウンでGeorge Campbell 13th of Loudounと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子の親であった。彼女は1494年3月9日に故郷で67歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley(이)가 1427년 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 더른리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Alan Stewart 2nd Lord of Aubigny and Concressault(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Catherine Seton(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 1444년 3월 29일 스컷랜드 에이르실르 루다운에서 George Campbell 13th of Loudoun(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1494년 3월 9일 그녀의 고향에서 67세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley nasceu em 1427, em Darnley, Renfrewshire, Scotland, seu pai, Alan Stewart 2nd Lord of Aubigny and Concressault, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Lady Catherine Seton, tinha 17 anos. Ela casou-se com George Campbell 13th of Loudoun em 29 de março de 1444, em Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos. Ela faleceu em 9 de março de 1494, em sua cidade natal, com 67 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley родилась в 1427 г. в местности дарнли, ренфрюшир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Alan Stewart 2nd Lord of Aubigny and Concressault было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Catherine Seton, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени George Campbell 13th of Loudoun 29 марта 1444 г. в местности лоудоун, аиршир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей. Она умерла 9 марта 1494 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 67 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Stewart of Darnley nació en 1427, en Darnley, Renfrewshire, Scotland, su papá, Alan Stewart 2nd Lord of Aubigny and Concressault, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Lady Catherine Seton, tenía 17 años. Se casó con George Campbell 13th of Loudoun el 29 de marzo de 1444, en Loudoun, Ayrshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos. Murió el 9 de marzo de 1494, en su pueblo, a la edad de 67 años.
